data.title: Figure, a publicly traded blockchain lender, reported a data breach after hackers targeted an employee in a social engineering attack.
data.description: A blockchain lender, Figure, disclosed that hackers were able to gain access to customer data following a social engineering attack on one of its employees. The breach resulted in unauthorized individuals potentially obtaining sensitive information of an undisclosed number of customers. Figure has taken steps to enhance its cybersecurity measures to prevent future incidents of this nature.

data.title: Retail sales stalled at year-end, with December sales flat compared to November, disappointing economists expecting a 0.4% gain. Some sectors, like furniture and electronics, experienced sales declines. This suggests a shift in consumer spending behavior.
data.description: Retail sales unexpectedly stalled at the end of the year in the US, sparking concerns about the American consumer's strength. Despite ongoing inflation and negative confidence surveys, consumer spending had continued to increase steadily in previous years. However, the latest data from the Commerce Department revealed stagnant retail sales in December, contrasting with economists' expectations of a 0.4% increase during the holiday season. The flat sales figure marked a clear departure from the consistent upward trend seen throughout most of the year, including a 0.6% rise in November and a 1% surge in June. The slowdown had been hinted at in earlier months, with slight declines in October and minimal growth in September. Data showed shoppers were increasingly selective, as sales dropped at furniture stores, electronics retailers, and restaurants. On the other hand, categories centered around essential items like building materials, gas stations, and food stores experienced stable sales.

data.title: White House celebrates 2 positive economic reports - strong jobs data and low inflation in January.
data.description: Positive employment figures and a slight decrease in inflation for January come as good news for President Trump. However, the overall economic outlook is less optimistic.

data.title: Trump Media's Truth Social applies for ETFs linked to Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Cronos. One each for Bitcoin/Ethereum and Cronos.
data.description: Truth Social Funds has submitted applications for exchange-traded funds (ETFs) aimed at providing investors with access to the crypto market. The ETFs would concentrate on two main cryptocurrencies - Bitcoin and Ethereum, as well as Cronos. This move by Truth Social Funds signifies a strategic initiative to offer investment opportunities in the rapidly growing digital assets space.

data.title: BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics awarded inducement grants on Feb 10, 2026, to two individuals hired in Jan 2026.
data.description: On February 10, 2026, BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics, Inc. (BBOT) based in South San Francisco made public the awarding of inducement grants under BBOT’s 2025 Inducement Plan. This announcement, made by the clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company specializing in RAS-pathway malignancies, was a significant move meant to attract and retain two employees hired in January 2026. The grants were a material inducement to their employment and were disclosed by BBOT on that day.

data.title: US Treasury Secretary believes enacting Clarity Act will stabilize markets amidst Bitcoin's volatility. #crypto #ClarityAct
data.description: The passage of the Clarity Act, as proposed by U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, is expected to bring a sense of calm to the cryptocurrency market. Bessent believes that this legislation will help clarify regulations in the industry, potentially stabilizing the market. This move could have a significant impact on the growing crypto market, which currently stands at a total market cap of over $2 trillion. By providing clarity and guidance, the Clarity Act aims to address regulatory uncertainties and promote a more secure environment for crypto investors and businesses.

data.title: Natural Gas Services Group, Inc. (NYSE: NGS) declares $0.11 quarterly dividend per share, $0.44 annually, up by 10% since 2025.
data.description: In Midland, Texas, on February 9, 2026, Natural Gas Services Group, Inc. (NYSE: NGS), a prominent provider of natural gas compression equipment and services to the energy sector, revealed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.11 per common stock share, totaling $0.44 annually. The dividend will be distributed on March 4, 2026, to shareholders noted by the close of business on February 18, 2026. This first quarter 2026 dividend mirrors the fourth quarter 2025 dividend, showing a 10% rise from the initial dividend payout in the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Apollo, $940B asset manager, ventures into crypto lending through Morpho agreement, eyeing up to 90M MORPHO tokens.
data.description: A prestigious asset management firm with assets totaling $940 billion has announced a partnership to develop DeFi lending infrastructure. As part of the collaboration, they are considering acquiring as many as 90 million MORPHO tokens.

data.title: Farmer Brothers Coffee reported Q2 fiscal 2026 results: $88.9M net sales, $32M gross profit, $4.9M net loss.
data.description: Farmer Brothers Coffee Co. reported its second quarter fiscal 2026 financial results, noting a challenging quarter where they experienced a decrease in net sales to $88.9 million, down by 1% compared to the previous year. Gross profit was $32 million, operating expenses were $36.4 million, and the company had a net loss of $4.9 million, compared to a net income of $200,000 in the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $484,000, down from $5.9 million. The company had $4.2 million in unrestricted cash and $24.6 million available under its revolver credit facility as of December 31, 2025. Farmer Brothers Coffee Co. will be hosting an investor conference call on February 13 to discuss these results further. The company's primary brands include Farmer Brothers, Boyd's Coffee, SUM>ONE Coffee Roasters, West Coast Coffee, Cain's, and China Mist.

data.title: NetActuate boosts Mumbai cloud platform with ONE capabilities, improving services in India, including Chennai PoP.
data.description: NetActuate, a prominent provider of global infrastructure and network services operating in over 45 markets worldwide, has recently enhanced its offerings in Mumbai and offers services from an established Point of Presence (PoP) in Chennai. This upgrade aims to improve performance, ensure compliance, and enhance connected architectures throughout India. The announcement was made in Mumbai, India, on February 15, 2026.
